mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS is positive, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market, leading to increased consumer confidence and spending, which boosts corporate profits and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.5% and 3-month average change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate has been elevated post-2008 crisis
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 7.5% and the 3-month average change is negative, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low and stable inflation with accommodative monetary policy (low federal funds rate) creates a favorable environment for equity valuations by reducing discount rates and supporting corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been near zero since 2008
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month change is below 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing and supply-side activity, reflecting robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3%, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts signals a rebound in the housing market and broader economic confidence, leading to increased construction activity and related spending, which provides a short-term boost to equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market was severely impacted by 2008 crisis
failure_condition: If HOUST 1-month percentage change exceeds 15%, but the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate earnings, consistently supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is a key indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If the 3-month average change in PAYEMS is positive, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment remains elevated despite very low federal funds rates, it signals persistent economic slack and aggressive monetary stimulus, which eventually supports asset prices by encouraging risk-taking and lowering the cost of capital, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Quantitative easing policies are in effect
failure_condition: If UNRATE is above 7.0% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed contraction in real GDP indicates a significant economic downturn, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and increased risk aversion among investors, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 1-quarter annualized growth is negative, but the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.
